perturbation.original_text:
9. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution.
9.1 This Agreement and any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter, existence, negotiation, validity, termination, breach or enforceability (including non-contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.
9.2 Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter, including any question regarding its existence, negotiation, termination, breach, validity or enforceability (including any non-contractual disputes or claims) ("Dispute") shall be referred to, and finally resolved by, arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration (the "LCIA Rules") of the London Court of International Arbitration (the "LCIA") which rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference into this Clause 9.2.
perturbation.changed_text:
7. Miscellaneous.
7.1 Nothing in this Agreement or in any document referred to in it shall constitute any of the Parties a partner of any other, nor shall the execution, completion and implementation of this Agreement confer on any Party any power to bind or impose any obligations to any third parties on any other Party or to pledge the credit of any other Party.
9. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution.
9.1 This Agreement and any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter, existence, negotiation, validity, termination, breach or enforceability (including non-contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.
9.2 Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter, including any question regarding its existence, negotiation, termination, breach, validity or enforceability (including any non-contractual disputes or claims) ("Dispute") shall be referred to, and finally resolved by, arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration (the "LCIA Rules") of the London Court of International Arbitration (the "LCIA") which rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference into this Clause 9.2.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law and dispute resolution clause (including mandatory arbitration) into the 'Miscellaneous' section makes it less prominent and potentially overlooked, hindering its enforceability. Placing the text in miscellaneous clauses can be seen as a way of concealing a dispute resolution agreement, making it less likely to be read by a signing party.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 3 of the UCTA 1977 addresses attempts to exclude or restrict liability for breach of contract. By burying the arbitration clause within 'Miscellaneous', a party could argue that the other party did not take reasonable steps to bring it to their attention, potentially rendering the arbitration clause subject to challenge under the UCTA. The UCTA imposes a reasonableness test on clauses excluding or limiting liability, and the placement of the arbitration clause affects whether this is met.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the change involves moving the governing law and dispute resolution clause, including mandatory arbitration, into the 'Miscellaneous' section, making it less prominent and potentially overlooked, hindering its enforceability. The law explanation states that by burying the arbitration clause within 'Miscellaneous', a party could argue that the other party did not take reasonable steps to bring it to their attention, potentially rendering the arbitration clause subject to challenge under the UCTA. This contradicts the enforceability of the agreement, aligning with the definition of a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction.
